results.title: Champions of Sport No.1
results.creator_credit: John Betts and Grand National Pictures
results.brief_description: This short film discusses the technique, history and famous sports-people of the 1940s, focusing on hurlding, golf and high jumping. The athletes spotlighted are D.O. Finlay (hurdler), Pam Barton (golfer) and C.C. Johnson (high jumper)

results.title: Champions of Sport No.4
results.creator_credit: John Betts and Grand National Pictures
results.brief_description: This film focuses on boxing, sculling and running. The correct techniques for boxing are demonstrated by Len Harvey - middle weight champion of Great Britain. In regards to sculling, Jack Beresford and Les Southwood, British Olympic champions, display the methodology. Finally the runner, Sidney Woodesden is shown, world record holder of the British mile and half-mile.

results.title: Chamberlain at Munich, September 29, 1938
results.creator_credit: Bernard Girard, James Fonda, Columbia Broadcasting System and Young America Films
results.brief_description: One of the “You are there” television series. This docu-drama takes its audience back to September 29th, 1938. It deals with the efforts of Britain’s Prime Minister, Neville Chamberlain, to preserve peace with Germany, and Adolf Hitler’s empty promise made in the Munich Pact. Captures the tension and fear which hung over Europe as Daladier and Chamberlain yielded to Hitler’s demands and signed the Munich Pact. Contains archival footage and a cast including Roland Varno, Noel Drayton, Mike McHale, Robert Burton and Stephen Bekassy. “All events reported and seen are based on historic fact and quotation.” Narrated by Walter Cronkite.

results.title: Challenging years
results.creator_credit: David E. Barrow, Peter Fletcher, Stanley Hawes, Motion Picture Associates and New South Wales Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Shows how some elderly men and women have planned for retirement. Illustrates, with dramatic vignettes, the positive and rewarding aspects of this period of life, and warns of the pitfalls to be avoided.

results.title: Challenge: Tasmania
results.creator_credit: Don Anderson, Ray Barnes and Tasmanian State Film Unit
results.brief_description: To all men the sea is a challenge, particularly to the yachtsmen who annually sail in the Sydney to Hobart ocean classic. 1971 was the final in the series for the Southern Cross Cup which was won by the New Zealanders. This film is a gripping account of the race.

results.title: Challenge: science against cancer
results.creator_credit: Morten Parker, Guy Glover and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: How far have scientists advanced toward solving the riddle of cancer, and what are the key problems now facing them? To explain the enormous complexity of the problem, the film traces briefly the growth and multiplication of a single fertilized cell into an adult man, and asks why some outlaw cells begin persistent growth after the whole body has reached maturity. Research scientists are shown following up clues with test tube, microscope, controlled diets and other aids that may guide them to the eventual solution. Methods of treatment being used meanwhile for different types of cancer are briefly described. As each new aspect of the disease is revealed, the secret of cancer is seen to be as complex as the universe itself, and the quest for its solution one of the greatest adventures on which a scientist can embark. Animation by Colin Low and Evelyn Lambart. Voice and narration by Raymond Massey. Awards: New York; Venice
